string-array.name: end_of_game_factoids
string-array.item: Someone born when the Wright Brothers made the first recorded flight in 1903 would have been just 65 years old when man landed on the moon.
Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found 3,000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still perfectly edible.
Octopuses have three hearts, nine brains, and blue blood.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 minutes, between Britain and Zanzibar in 1896.
Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
The Eiffel Tower can be 15 cm taller during the summer due to thermal expansion of the iron.
Venus is the only planet that spins clockwise.
Scotland\'s national animal is the unicorn.
A single cloud can weigh more than a million pounds.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than there are atoms in the known universe.
The total weight of all ants on Earth is roughly equal to the total weight of all humans.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid of Giza.
An octopus has no bones, allowing it to squeeze through any gap larger than its beak.
Light takes 8 minutes and 20 seconds to travel from the Sun to Earth.
The inventor of the Pringles can is buried in one.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time: one hundredth of a second.
The average person walks the equivalent of three times around the world in a lifetime.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
The heart of a blue whale is so big that a small child could swim through its arteries.
Sharks are older than trees. Sharks have existed for around 400 million years, while trees have only been around for 350 million.
A teaspoonful of neutron star would weigh about 6 billion tons.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye, but highways are.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when they are separated.
The national anthem of Spain has no words.
Wombat poop is cube-shaped to stop it from rolling away.
The average cumulus cloud weighs about 1.1 million pounds.
More people are killed each year by vending machines than by sharks.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The inventor of the fire hydrant is unknown because the patent was destroyed in a fire.
Humans share about 60% of their DNA with bananas.
The longest hiccuping spree lasted 68 years.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all the beaches on Earth.
Nintendo was founded in 1889, originally as a playing card company.
Hot water freezes faster than cold water. This is known as the Mpemba effect.
The tongue is the strongest muscle in the human body relative to its size.
A bolt of lightning is five times hotter than the surface of the Sun.
The shortest complete sentence in English is "Go."
Butterflies taste with their feet.
The dot over the letters i and j is called a tittle.
A group of porcupines is called a prickle.
The longest word you can type with only your left hand on a standard keyboard is "stewardesses."
Sloths can hold their breath longer than dolphins — up to 40 minutes.
The longest place name in the world is 85 letters long and is located in New Zealand.
A snail can sleep for three years.
The moon has moonquakes, similar to earthquakes on Earth.
Astronauts grow up to 2 inches taller in space because of the lack of gravity.
The fingerprints of a koala are so similar to humans that they have confused crime scene investigators.
Apples float in water because they are 25% air.
The longest recorded flight of a chicken is 13 seconds.
There are more fake flamingos in the world than real ones.
A crocodile cannot stick its tongue out.
An average person produces enough saliva in their lifetime to fill two swimming pools.
The inventor of the microwave oven only received $2 for his discovery.
A photon takes about 40,000 years to travel from the core of the Sun to its surface, but only 8 minutes to reach Earth.
Polar bears have black skin under their white fur.
The Hawaiian pizza was invented in Canada.
A blue whale\'s heartbeat can be detected from two miles away.
The smallest bone in the human body is in the ear and is called the stapes.
There are more trees on Earth than stars in the Milky Way.
Goldfish have a memory span of at least three months, not three seconds.
The electric chair was invented by a dentist.
Peanuts are not nuts. They are legumes.
A flea can jump up to 150 times its own body length.
The oldest known living tree is over 5,000 years old.
The total length of all the blood vessels in the human body is about 60,000 miles.
A group of owls is called a parliament.
The average person spends two weeks of their lifetime waiting for traffic lights to change.
Dolphins sleep with one eye open.
The first computer bug was an actual bug — a moth found in a Harvard computer in 1947.
Honey bees can recognize human faces.
The driest place on Earth is in Antarctica, not the Sahara.
A rhinoceros\'s horn is made of hair.
The chance of being born on February 29th is about 1 in 1,461.
Roman gladiators rarely fought to the death — they were too expensive to train.
The speed of a computer mouse is measured in Mickeys.
An ostrich\'s eye is bigger than its brain.
If you shuffle a deck of cards properly, the resulting order has probably never existed before.
The Sahara Desert is roughly the same size as the United States.
Crows can remember human faces and hold grudges.
The average lightning bolt could power a toaster for two months.
Seahorses are the only animals where the male gives birth.
The human nose can detect over 1 trillion different scents.
Rubber bands last longer when refrigerated.
A group of jellyfish is called a smack.
The longest English word without a vowel is "rhythms."
Elephants are the only animals that cannot jump.
The average pencil can draw a line 35 miles long.
A cockroach can live for a week without its head before dying of starvation.
The Mona Lisa has no eyebrows — it was fashionable to shave them in Renaissance Florence.
Sound travels about 4 times faster in water than in air.
The longest tennis match lasted 11 hours and 5 minutes over three days at Wimbledon in 2010.
Alaska is simultaneously the most northern, western, and eastern state in the United States.
The first oranges weren\'t orange — they were green.
Your body produces enough heat in 30 minutes to boil half a gallon of water.
A cat has 32 muscles in each ear.
The total surface area of human lungs is roughly equal to a tennis court.
Babies are born with about 300 bones, but adults only have 206.
A hummingbird weighs less than a penny.
The longest word in the English language with all its letters in alphabetical order is "almost."
More than 80% of the ocean is unexplored.
A single strand of spider silk is thinner than a human hair but stronger than steel of the same weight.
The King of Hearts is the only king in a deck of cards without a moustache.
Earth\'s rotation is gradually slowing — days were only about 6 hours long 4.5 billion years ago.
Pigeons can do math. They can learn abstract numerical rules.
The first alarm clock could only ring at 4 AM.
A rainbow seen from an airplane is a full circle.
The word "nerd" was first coined by Dr. Seuss in the book "If I Ran the Zoo" in 1950.
Every two minutes, we take more photos than all of humanity did in the 1800s.
Cats have fewer toes on their back paws than their front paws.
The shortest commercial flight in the world lasts about 57 seconds between two Scottish islands.
Cinnamon comes from the bark of a tree.
A bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
The average cloud moves at about 30 miles per hour.
Greenland sharks can live for over 400 years.
The sum of all numbers on a roulette wheel is 666.
Armadillos nearly always give birth to identical quadruplets.
A single Google search uses about 0.3 watt-hours of electricity.
Bees can fly higher than Mount Everest.
The plastic tips on shoelaces are called aglets.
A mantis shrimp can punch with the force of a bullet.
There are more possible games of chess than atoms in the observable universe.
The longest hiccuping spree was experienced by Charles Osborne, who hiccupped for 68 years straight.
Avocados are toxic to birds.
The average person will spend six months of their life waiting for red lights to turn green.
In Switzerland, it is illegal to own just one guinea pig because they get lonely.
A group of crows is called a murder.
The human eye can distinguish about 10 million different colors.
The shortest war in recorded history was 38 to 45 minutes long between Britain and Zanzibar.
Coca-Cola would be green if food coloring was not added.
The world\'s oldest piece of chewing gum is over 9,000 years old.
A bolt of lightning can reach temperatures of roughly 30,000 Kelvin.
Venus rotates so slowly that a single day on Venus lasts 243 Earth days.
Humans glow in the dark, but the light is too faint for the human eye to detect.
The Pacific Ocean is larger than all the land on Earth combined.
Honey is the only food that includes all the substances necessary to sustain life.
There are about 10 quintillion insects alive on Earth at any given time.
The average human body contains enough iron to make a 3-inch nail.
Tardigrades can survive in the vacuum of space.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time — it is one-hundredth of a second.
Cats spend about 70% of their lives sleeping.
The unicorn is Scotland\'s national animal.
A flock of ravens is called an unkindness.
The average person produces about 25,000 quarts of saliva in a lifetime.
You are about 1 cm taller in the morning than in the evening.
The last letter added to the English alphabet was J.
Approximately 10% of all photographs ever taken were taken in the last 12 months.
A group of cats is called a clowder.
The space between your eyebrows is called the glabella.
Dragonflies have existed for over 300 million years.
There is a species of jellyfish that is biologically immortal.
The average person walks about 100,000 miles in their lifetime.
The longest bridge in the world is over 100 miles long and is in China.
Pigs cannot look up at the sky while standing.
A woodpecker\'s tongue wraps around the back of its skull.
The world\'s deepest postbox is in Susami Bay, Japan, at 10 meters underwater.
A chameleon\'s tongue can be twice as long as its body.
The longest nonstop flight by a bird lasted 11 days — achieved by the bar-tailed godwit.
Saturn would float if you could find a bathtub big enough because it is less dense than water.
The word "set" has the most definitions of any English word.
Lobsters taste with their feet.
A group of hippos is called a bloat.
Your stomach gets a new lining every three to four days.
The average person blinks about 15 to 20 times per minute.
The Amazon River once flowed in the opposite direction, from east to west.
Platypuses are venomous mammals.
The longest time between two twins being born is 87 days.
A housefly hums in the key of F.
The inventor of the Segway died by riding a Segway off a cliff.
Earth has had five mass extinction events.
A giraffe\'s tongue is about 18 to 20 inches long.
There are about 8.7 million species on Earth, and over 80% have not yet been discovered.
An ant can lift 50 times its own body weight.
The oldest known joke dates back to 1900 BC from ancient Sumer.
Mercury and Venus are the only planets in our solar system that have no moons.
A piece of paper can be folded no more than 7 times by hand.
The yo-yo is one of the oldest toys in the world, dating back to 500 BC in Greece.
Bananas are naturally slightly radioactive due to their potassium content.
A single lightning strike can power a 100-watt light bulb for about 3 months.
There are more atoms in a glass of water than glasses of water in all the oceans.
Starfish do not have brains.
The first computer programmer was Ada Lovelace, who wrote the first algorithm in the 1840s.
Ants do not have lungs — they breathe through tiny holes in their body called spiracles.
A newborn kangaroo is about the size of a lima bean.
Humans and giraffes have the same number of neck vertebrae — seven.
The lighter was invented before the match.
A ball of glass will bounce higher than a ball of rubber.
The lifespan of a taste bud is about 10 days.
Canada has more lakes than the rest of the world\'s countries combined.
The word "alphabet" comes from the first two letters of the Greek alphabet: alpha and beta.
There is a town in Norway called Hell, and it freezes over every winter.
Otters hold hands while sleeping so they don\'t drift apart.
The first recorded use of OMG was in a 1917 letter to Winston Churchill.
A day on Mercury lasts 59 Earth days.
Koalas sleep up to 22 hours a day.
The deepest point in the ocean is about 36,000 feet below the surface.
The original name for the search engine Google was Backrub.
A cubic inch of human bone can bear a load of about 19,000 pounds.
The Voyager 1 spacecraft is the farthest human-made object from Earth.
Dolphins have names for each other and respond when called.
A bolt of lightning can strike the same place twice — the Empire State Building is hit about 23 times per year.
The total weight of all the bacteria in your body is about 2 to 5 pounds.
The oldest known recipe is for beer, from ancient Sumeria around 4,000 years ago.
Spiders can survive underwater for hours by trapping air bubbles around their bodies.
Horses can sleep standing up thanks to a special locking mechanism in their legs.
It rains diamonds on Jupiter and Saturn.
The world\'s smallest mammal is the bumblebee bat, weighing less than a penny.
Your brain uses about 20% of your body\'s total energy despite being only 2% of your body weight.
The Statue of Liberty\'s index finger is 8 feet long.
Octopuses have been observed using coconut shells as portable shelters.
A cubic mile of seawater contains about 25 pounds of gold.
There are about 200 corpses on Mount Everest, and they are used as trail markers.
The strongest muscle in the human body is the masseter, or jaw muscle.
Cows produce more milk when listening to slow music.
A frog can\'t swallow without blinking because it uses its eyes to push food down its throat.
The inventor of the Pringles container was buried in one per his wishes.
A caterpillar has more muscles than a human — about 4,000 compared to roughly 650.
Lake Baikal in Russia contains about 20% of the world\'s unfrozen fresh water.
There are more ways to arrange a deck of cards than there are atoms on Earth.
The first toy to be advertised on television was Mr. Potato Head.
Giraffes only need about 30 minutes of sleep per day.
The planet Uranus was originally going to be called Georgium Sidus, after King George III.
A mosquito has 47 teeth.
The smell of freshly cut grass is actually a plant distress signal.
Sunflowers follow the sun across the sky, a behavior called heliotropism.
You can hear a blue whale\'s heartbeat from over 2 miles away.
The first email was sent in 1971, but nobody remembers what it said.
Only female mosquitoes bite — they need the protein from blood for their eggs.
The largest living organism is a honey fungus in Oregon spanning 2,385 acres.
A person\'s DNA could stretch from the Sun to Pluto and back 17 times if uncoiled.
The word "lollipop" was added to the Oxford English Dictionary in 1784.
Ancient Romans used crushed mouse brains as toothpaste.
Flamingos can only eat with their heads upside down.
There are about 7,000 languages spoken in the world today.
The Earth weighs approximately 13.17 septillion pounds.
A sneeze travels at about 100 miles per hour.
Cleopatra spoke at least nine languages.
The color orange was named after the fruit, not the other way around.
There is a basketball court on the top floor of the U.S. Supreme Court Building.
A group of parrots is called a pandemonium.
The average person has about 67 different species of bacteria in their belly button.
Glaciers and ice sheets hold about 69% of the world\'s freshwater.
A cockroach can hold its breath for 40 minutes.
The Moon is slowly drifting away from Earth at about 1.5 inches per year.
Turtles can breathe through their rear ends.
The shortest verse in the Bible is "Jesus wept."
Your corneas are the only part of your body that has no blood supply — they get oxygen directly from the air.
A single teaspoon of soil contains more organisms than there are people on Earth.
The world record for the most T-shirts worn at once is 260.
Pineapples take about two years to grow.
Astronaut footprints on the Moon will remain there for at least 100 million years.
